afghanistan.name: Afghanistan
afghanistan.fact: Afghanistan has been invaded by basically every empire in history, each one leaving confused and slightly poorer.

albania.name: Albania
albania.fact: Albania has more Mercedes-Benz cars per capita than almost any country on Earth, despite being one of Europe's poorest nations.

algeria.name: Algeria
algeria.fact: Algeria is the largest country in Africa, yet 90% of its population lives on just 12% of the land because the rest is the Sahara Desert.

andorra.name: Andorra
andorra.fact: Andorra has no army and hasn't fought a war since 1278, making it the world's most successful pacifist microstate run by two co-princes.

angola.name: Angola
angola.fact: Luanda was once ranked the most expensive city in the world for expats, yet most locals live on under $2 a day.

antigua_and_barbuda.name: Antigua and Barbuda
antigua_and_barbuda.fact: Antigua boasts 365 beaches, one for every day of the year, which is either brilliant marketing or a very suspicious coincidence.

argentina.name: Argentina
argentina.fact: Argentina has had so many economic crises that its citizens are basically professional financial disaster survivors.

armenia.name: Armenia
armenia.fact: Armenia claims to be the world's first Christian nation, which they bring up approximately every 10 minutes in conversation.

australia.name: Australia
australia.fact: Australia lost a war to emus in 1932 and the emus won. This is an actual historical event the Australian government prefers not to discuss.

austria.name: Austria
austria.fact: Austria gave the world both Mozart and Hitler, and Austrians have been trying to balance that cultural ledger ever since.

azerbaijan.name: Azerbaijan
azerbaijan.fact: Azerbaijan has mud volcanoes that occasionally catch fire, creating flaming mud geysers that are somehow not the country's biggest tourist attraction.

bahamas.name: Bahamas
bahamas.fact: The Bahamas has an island called Pig Beach populated entirely by swimming pigs who will aggressively demand your lunch.

bahrain.name: Bahrain
bahrain.fact: Bahrain is so small you can drive across the entire country in 30 minutes, yet it somehow hosts a Formula 1 Grand Prix.

bangladesh.name: Bangladesh
bangladesh.fact: Dhaka is one of the most densely populated cities on Earth, where personal space is considered a Western luxury.

barbados.name: Barbados
barbados.fact: Barbados is the birthplace of rum and takes this responsibility extremely seriously, with more rum shops per square mile than anywhere on Earth.

belarus.name: Belarus
belarus.fact: Belarus is often called Europe's last dictatorship, which is harsh but the current president has been in power since 1994 so it's hard to argue.

belgium.name: Belgium
belgium.fact: Belgium once went 589 days without a government and nobody really noticed because the bureaucracy just kept running itself.

belize.name: Belize
belize.fact: Belize moved its capital after a hurricane destroyed the old one, making it one of the few countries to relocate due to bad weather.

benin.name: Benin
benin.fact: Benin is considered the birthplace of Voodoo, the actual religious practice, not the Hollywood version involving dolls and pins.

bhutan.name: Bhutan
bhutan.fact: Bhutan measures its success in Gross National Happiness instead of GDP, which sounds lovely until you realise tourism is strictly limited and expensive.

bolivia.name: Bolivia
bolivia.fact: Bolivia has two capitals: Sucre is the constitutional one and La Paz is the actual working one, because one capital is simply not enough confusion.

bosnia_and_herzegovina.name: Bosnia and Herzegovina
bosnia_and_herzegovina.fact: Bosnia and Herzegovina has three presidents who rotate every 8 months, which is either very democratic or absolutely chaotic.

botswana.name: Botswana
botswana.fact: Botswana went from one of the world's poorest countries at independence to an upper-middle-income nation, largely thanks to diamonds and competent governance.

brazil.name: Brazil
brazil.fact: Brazil built an entirely new capital city from scratch in just 41 months, then made it look like a spaceship from above because why not.

brunei.name: Brunei
brunei.fact: The Sultan of Brunei owns over 7,000 cars including 600 Rolls-Royces, which raises questions about where he parks them all.

bulgaria.name: Bulgaria
bulgaria.fact: Bulgarians shake their head to mean yes and nod to mean no, which has caused enormous confusion for every tourist who has ever been there.

burkina_faso.name: Burkina Faso
burkina_faso.fact: Burkina Faso's capital Ouagadougou is widely considered one of the most fun city names to say out loud, which is genuinely one of its best features.

burundi.name: Burundi
burundi.fact: Burundi changed its capital from Bujumbura to Gitega in 2019, which is remarkable mainly because Bujumbura is a much more satisfying word.

cabo_verde.name: Cabo Verde
cabo_verde.fact: Cabo Verde has no indigenous population — everyone there is descended from Portuguese colonists and African slaves, making it one of the world's most intentionally created nations.

cambodia.name: Cambodia
cambodia.fact: Cambodia is the only country whose flag features a building, Angkor Wat, which is fair enough as it's genuinely spectacular.

cameroon.name: Cameroon
cameroon.fact: Cameroon is called Africa in miniature because it contains basically every climate and landscape the continent has to offer, crammed into one country.

canada.name: Canada
canada.fact: Canada has more lakes than the rest of the world combined and somehow still manages to be polite about it.

central_african_republic.name: Central African Republic
central_african_republic.fact: The CAR once had an emperor who crowned himself in a $30 million ceremony, which was roughly the country's entire annual budget.

chad.name: Chad
chad.fact: Chad's flag is almost identical to Romania's flag, a situation both countries are apparently fine with since neither has done anything about it.

chile.name: Chile
chile.fact: Chile is the longest and narrowest country in the world — 4,300km long but only 350km wide — like someone stretched it on a rack.

china.name: China
china.fact: China has a single time zone despite spanning five geographical ones, meaning sunrise in western China can happen at 10am.

colombia.name: Colombia
colombia.fact: Colombia is the only country in South America with coastlines on both the Pacific Ocean and the Caribbean Sea, which it is very smug about.

comoros.name: Comoros
comoros.fact: Comoros has had over 20 coups or coup attempts since independence in 1975, averaging roughly one coup every two years.

congo_republic.name: Congo, Republic of the
congo_republic.fact: Brazzaville and Kinshasa are two capitals directly facing each other across the Congo River, making them the closest pair of capitals in the world.

congo_dr.name: Congo, Democratic Republic of the
congo_dr.fact: The DRC contains enough hydroelectric potential to power the entire African continent, yet most of its citizens have no electricity.

costa_rica.name: Costa Rica
costa_rica.fact: Costa Rica abolished its military in 1948 and spent the defence budget on education and healthcare instead, which turned out to be a great decision.

croatia.name: Croatia
croatia.fact: Croatia invented the necktie, which means every office worker silently cursing their Monday morning outfit has Croatia to blame.

cuba.name: Cuba
cuba.fact: Cuba has one of the highest doctor-to-patient ratios in the world and exports doctors to other countries, which is either admirable or suspicious.

cyprus.name: Cyprus
cyprus.fact: Nicosia is the last divided capital city in the world, split by a UN buffer zone that cuts straight through the city centre.

czech_republic.name: Czech Republic
czech_republic.fact: The Czech Republic drinks more beer per capita than any other country on Earth, which they consider a point of national pride.

denmark.name: Denmark
denmark.fact: Denmark consistently ranks as the happiest country in the world despite weather that would make anyone miserable, suggesting Danes are either lying or very good at hygge.

djibouti.name: Djibouti
djibouti.fact: Djibouti hosts military bases from the USA, France, China, Japan and Italy simultaneously, making it the world's most diplomatically crowded small nation.

dominica.name: Dominica
dominica.fact: Dominica's flag features the Sisserou parrot, making it one of the very few national flags in the world to feature the colour purple.

dominican_republic.name: Dominican Republic
dominican_republic.fact: The Dominican Republic shares the island of Hispaniola with Haiti, making it one of only a handful of islands shared by two completely different countries.

ecuador.name: Ecuador
ecuador.fact: Ecuador is named after the equator but the actual equator doesn't pass through the capital Quito, which is a geographical embarrassment nobody mentions.

egypt.name: Egypt
egypt.fact: Egypt is 96% desert, meaning almost the entire population of 100 million people is crammed along a thin strip of land beside the Nile.

el_salvador.name: El Salvador
el_salvador.fact: El Salvador became the first country to adopt Bitcoin as legal tender in 2021, a decision that has not gone entirely as planned.

equatorial_guinea.name: Equatorial Guinea
equatorial_guinea.fact: Equatorial Guinea is the only Spanish-speaking country in Africa, which confuses people geographically and linguistically in equal measure.

eritrea.name: Eritrea
eritrea.fact: Eritrea is sometimes called the North Korea of Africa due to its closed-off nature, which the government presumably considers a compliment.

estonia.name: Estonia
estonia.fact: Estonia is so digitally advanced you can vote, file taxes, start a business and access medical records online — basically everything except get a coffee.

eswatini.name: Eswatini
eswatini.fact: Eswatini is one of the world's last absolute monarchies, and the king renamed the country on his 50th birthday because it was his country and he felt like it.

ethiopia.name: Ethiopia
ethiopia.fact: Ethiopia has its own calendar with 13 months and is currently about 7 years behind the rest of the world — Ethiopians are literally living in the past.

fiji.name: Fiji
fiji.fact: Fiji is made up of 332 islands but only 110 are inhabited, leaving over 200 perfectly good islands sitting empty like unsold houses.

finland.name: Finland
finland.fact: Finland has the most heavy metal bands per capita of any country in the world, which seems at odds with their reputation for quiet, introverted politeness.

france.name: France
france.fact: France is the most visited country in the world, yet the French have somehow maintained an air of mild contempt for tourists throughout.

gabon.name: Gabon
gabon.fact: Gabon has declared it will never cut more forest than it regrows, making it one of the few carbon-negative nations on Earth.

gambia.name: Gambia
gambia.fact: Gambia is almost entirely surrounded by Senegal, like a country that got swallowed by another country.

georgia.name: Georgia
georgia.fact: Georgia claims to be the birthplace of wine, with evidence going back 8,000 years, which explains why they take viticulture so personally.

germany.name: Germany
germany.fact: Germany has over 1,500 different types of beer and 300 types of bread, suggesting Germans have very strong opinions about carbohydrates.

ghana.name: Ghana
ghana.fact: Ghana was the first sub-Saharan African country to gain independence and named itself after a medieval empire that wasn't even in the same location, for inspirational purposes.

greece.name: Greece
greece.fact: Greece has the longest coastline in the Mediterranean yet its economy has spent most of the 21st century underwater, which is ironic.

grenada.name: Grenada
grenada.fact: Grenada produces roughly 20% of the world's nutmeg, which is a lot of nutmeg for a country of 100,000 people.

guatemala.name: Guatemala
guatemala.fact: Guatemala has 37 volcanoes, three of which are currently active, suggesting Guatemalans have a very relaxed relationship with geological risk.

guinea.name: Guinea
guinea.fact: Guinea contains over half of the world's bauxite reserves yet remains one of the world's poorest countries.

guinea_bissau.name: Guinea-Bissau
guinea_bissau.fact: Guinea-Bissau has had more successful coups than democratic elections since independence, making it something of a world leader in political instability.

guyana.name: Guyana
guyana.fact: Guyana is the only English-speaking country in South America, which means it gets left off South American maps with suspicious frequency.

haiti.name: Haiti
haiti.fact: Haiti was the first country founded by formerly enslaved people after a successful revolution, which France then charged them reparations for.

honduras.name: Honduras
honduras.fact: Honduras and El Salvador once fought a war triggered by a football match, proving sports rivalries can have catastrophic consequences.

hungary.name: Hungary
hungary.fact: Hungary invented the Rubik's Cube, the ballpoint pen, and the hologram, making it responsible for three of history's most mind-bending inventions.

iceland.name: Iceland
iceland.fact: Iceland has no mosquitoes whatsoever, making it genuinely one of the most pleasant countries to visit in summer without dousing yourself in DEET.

india.name: India
india.fact: India has 22 official languages, and the government's paperwork is available in all of them, which is both impressive and a logistical nightmare.

indonesia.name: Indonesia
indonesia.fact: Indonesia is moving its capital from Jakarta to a brand new city because Jakarta is sinking into the sea at an alarming rate.

iran.name: Iran
iran.fact: Iran invented chess, algebra and the postage stamp, yet somehow the postage stamp is the one that never gets mentioned in history class.

iraq.name: Iraq
iraq.fact: Iraq is home to Mesopotamia, the Cradle of Civilisation, where humans invented writing, the wheel and cities — an impressive hat-trick.

ireland.name: Ireland
ireland.fact: Ireland has more Nobel Prize winners per capita than almost any other country, which surprised everyone including the Irish.

israel.name: Israel
israel.fact: Israel has the highest number of museums per capita in the world, suggesting nobody can agree on what to do with all the history.

italy.name: Italy
italy.fact: Italy has more UNESCO World Heritage Sites than any other country, meaning Italians are essentially living inside a museum they're not allowed to renovate.

jamaica.name: Jamaica
jamaica.fact: Jamaica has never won a Winter Olympics gold medal but dominates the Summer Olympics in sprinting, proving climate is destiny.

japan.name: Japan
japan.fact: Japan has vending machines that sell everything from hot meals to neckties, meaning you could theoretically survive entirely on vending machine purchases.

jordan.name: Jordan
jordan.fact: Jordan is home to the Dead Sea, the lowest point on Earth, where you float effortlessly because the water is so salty it essentially refuses to let you drown.

kazakhstan.name: Kazakhstan
kazakhstan.fact: Kazakhstan is the world's largest landlocked country and has renamed its capital three times in 25 years, suggesting a deep national uncertainty about identity.

kenya.name: Kenya
kenya.fact: Kenya has produced some of the greatest long-distance runners in history, leading scientists to study whether the entire country is simply built differently.

kiribati.name: Kiribati
kiribati.fact: Kiribati is the first country to see each new year and also the first likely to disappear due to rising sea levels, making it symbolically significant in two very different ways.

kosovo.name: Kosovo
kosovo.fact: Kosovo declared independence in 2008 and is recognised by about 100 countries, meaning roughly 90 countries still pretend it doesn't exist.

kuwait.name: Kuwait
kuwait.fact: Kuwait has no income tax, no sales tax and heavily subsidised petrol, which sounds perfect until you check the summer temperature of 50 degrees.

kyrgyzstan.name: Kyrgyzstan
kyrgyzstan.fact: Kyrgyzstan's flag features a yurt's roof viewed from the inside, making it possibly the only flag designed from the perspective of someone lying in a tent.

laos.name: Laos
laos.fact: Laos is the most heavily bombed country per capita in history, with the US dropping more bombs on it than on Germany and Japan combined in WWII.

latvia.name: Latvia
latvia.fact: Latvia's flag is one of the oldest in the world, with records of its use going back to 1280, predating most countries that use it as inspiration.

lebanon.name: Lebanon
lebanon.fact: Lebanon has 18 officially recognised religious sects and allocates government positions by religion, which works about as well as you'd expect.

lesotho.name: Lesotho
lesotho.fact: Lesotho is one of only three countries entirely surrounded by a single other country — South Africa — making it the world's most surrounded nation.

liberia.name: Liberia
liberia.fact: Liberia was founded by freed American slaves in 1847 and modelled its constitution on the United States, then proceeded to have a completely different experience.

libya.name: Libya
libya.fact: Libya had the world's simplest national flag from 1977 to 2011 — just a solid green rectangle — which required very little design effort but a lot of explanation.

liechtenstein.name: Liechtenstein
liechtenstein.fact: Liechtenstein discovered at the 1936 Olympics that its flag was identical to Haiti's, so it added a crown — suggesting nobody had checked beforehand.

lithuania.name: Lithuania
lithuania.fact: Lithuania was the last country in Europe to officially convert to Christianity, holding out until 1387, which is impressively stubborn.

luxembourg.name: Luxembourg
luxembourg.fact: Luxembourg has the highest GDP per capita in the world and its citizens have apparently agreed not to talk about this too loudly in front of their neighbours.

madagascar.name: Madagascar
madagascar.fact: Over 90% of Madagascar's wildlife is found nowhere else on Earth, making it essentially a separate evolutionary experiment on the same planet.

malawi.name: Malawi
malawi.fact: Malawi is called the Warm Heart of Africa due to its exceptionally friendly population, which is a nicer national brand than most countries manage.

malaysia.name: Malaysia
malaysia.fact: Malaysia's flag looks remarkably like the American flag, which is either a coincidence or a very bold design choice that nobody has questioned loudly enough.

maldives.name: Maldives
maldives.fact: The Maldives is the lowest-lying country in the world at 2.4 metres maximum elevation, meaning a slightly ambitious wave could theoretically end the nation.

mali.name: Mali
mali.fact: Mali was once home to Timbuktu, a medieval centre of scholarship so remote that 'from here to Timbuktu' became synonymous with the middle of nowhere.

malta.name: Malta
malta.fact: Valletta is the smallest EU capital and has more UNESCO World Heritage Sites per square metre than anywhere else on Earth.

marshall_islands.name: Marshall Islands
marshall_islands.fact: The Marshall Islands had 67 nuclear weapons detonated on it by the United States between 1946 and 1958, which the Marshall Islands considers a significant overstep.

mauritania.name: Mauritania
mauritania.fact: Mauritania only criminalised slavery in 2007, making it one of the last countries on Earth to take that particular step.

mauritius.name: Mauritius
mauritius.fact: Mauritius was the only known home of the dodo bird, which it managed to drive to extinction within 80 years of first encountering it.

mexico.name: Mexico
mexico.fact: Mexico City is built on an ancient lake bed, which is why it sinks about 50cm every year and experiences earthquakes with unusual enthusiasm.

micronesia.name: Micronesia
micronesia.fact: Micronesia uses the US dollar as its currency but has no say in US monetary policy, which is an arrangement the US finds perfectly reasonable.

moldova.name: Moldova
moldova.fact: Moldova is consistently ranked as Europe's poorest country, yet somehow produces some of the continent's most celebrated wine.

monaco.name: Monaco
monaco.fact: Monaco is the second smallest country in the world and the most densely populated, meaning its entire population could fit in a large shopping centre.

mongolia.name: Mongolia
mongolia.fact: Mongolia is the most sparsely populated country in the world with roughly 2 people per square kilometre, meaning the horses outnumber the humans.

montenegro.name: Montenegro
montenegro.fact: Montenegro adopted the Euro without being in the Eurozone or the EU, which the European Central Bank finds both flattering and slightly irritating.

morocco.name: Morocco
morocco.fact: Morocco is home to the University of Al Quaraouiyine, founded in 859 AD, widely considered the world's oldest continuously operating university.

mozambique.name: Mozambique
mozambique.fact: Mozambique's flag features an AK-47 assault rifle, making it the only national flag to depict a specific modern weapon.

myanmar.name: Myanmar
myanmar.fact: Myanmar built a brand new capital in the middle of nowhere in 2006, reportedly because a military astrologer told the generals to move.

namibia.name: Namibia
namibia.fact: Namibia has one of the lowest population densities in the world, meaning you could drive for hours without seeing another human being.

nauru.name: Nauru
nauru.fact: Nauru got extremely rich from phosphate mining, blew all the money, and now survives partly by selling passports and hosting offshore detention centres.

nepal.name: Nepal
nepal.fact: Nepal is the only country with a non-rectangular flag — it's a double pennant shape that required a special mathematical formula to standardise.

netherlands.name: Netherlands
netherlands.fact: A quarter of the Netherlands sits below sea level, held back by dykes that the Dutch maintain with impressive engineering and quiet anxiety.

new_zealand.name: New Zealand
new_zealand.fact: New Zealand was the first country to give women the right to vote in 1893, and has spent 130 years wondering why everyone else took so long.

nicaragua.name: Nicaragua
nicaragua.fact: Nicaragua is the largest country in Central America yet somehow the least visited, which its tourism board is working very hard to change.

niger.name: Niger
niger.fact: Niger is the largest country in West Africa with over 80% of its land covered by the Sahara Desert, leaving not much room for anything else.

nigeria.name: Nigeria
nigeria.fact: Nigeria has the second largest film industry in the world by volume, producing more films annually than Hollywood.

north_korea.name: North Korea
north_korea.fact: North Korea created its own time zone called Pyongyang Time in 2015 to mark liberation from Japan, then quietly reversed it in 2018.

north_macedonia.name: North Macedonia
north_macedonia.fact: North Macedonia had to add the word North to its name after a 27-year dispute with Greece about who owns the historical brand of Macedonia.

norway.name: Norway
norway.fact: Norway has a sovereign wealth fund worth over a trillion dollars, meaning every Norwegian citizen is technically a millionaire on paper.

oman.name: Oman
oman.fact: Oman is one of the most stable countries in the Middle East, achieved through oil wealth, diplomacy and not picking fights with anyone.

pakistan.name: Pakistan
pakistan.fact: Pakistan is home to K2, the second highest and most dangerous mountain in the world, which Pakistan is oddly proud of.

palau.name: Palau
palau.fact: Palau has a lake full of non-stinging jellyfish you can swim through, which sounds like a fever dream but is a genuine tourist attraction.

panama.name: Panama
panama.fact: Panama hats are not from Panama — they're from Ecuador. They got the name because they were shipped through Panama during canal construction.

papua_new_guinea.name: Papua New Guinea
papua_new_guinea.fact: Papua New Guinea has over 800 languages within its borders, representing roughly 12% of all languages on Earth.

paraguay.name: Paraguay
paraguay.fact: Paraguay has different emblems on each side of its flag, making it one of the very few non-symmetrical national flags in the world.

peru.name: Peru
peru.fact: Peru is home to Machu Picchu, built at 2,430 metres altitude without wheels, iron tools or mortar, just to make modern architects feel inadequate.

philippines.name: Philippines
philippines.fact: The Philippines flag is flown upside down during wartime as a built-in distress signal, which is either clever or an accident waiting to happen.

poland.name: Poland
poland.fact: Poland has been invaded, partitioned and erased from maps multiple times throughout history, yet keeps coming back, making it Europe's most resilient nation.

portugal.name: Portugal
portugal.fact: Portugal is the oldest nation-state in Europe with borders essentially unchanged since 1139, which it maintains is stability rather than stubbornness.

qatar.name: Qatar
qatar.fact: Qatar spent $220 billion hosting a football World Cup, including air-conditioning outdoor stadiums in 40 degree heat.

romania.name: Romania
romania.fact: Romania has Dracula, Nadia Comaneci and the world's heaviest building, which required demolishing a fifth of Bucharest's old city to construct.

russia.name: Russia
russia.fact: Russia spans 11 time zones, meaning you can celebrate New Year's Eve 11 times in a single trip across the country if you move fast enough.

rwanda.name: Rwanda
rwanda.fact: Rwanda has a national day of community cleaning called Umuganda, and plastic bags are completely banned, making it one of Africa's cleanest countries.

saint_kitts_and_nevis.name: Saint Kitts and Nevis
saint_kitts_and_nevis.fact: Saint Kitts and Nevis is the smallest sovereign state in the Western hemisphere, yet has a full seat at the United Nations.

saint_lucia.name: Saint Lucia
saint_lucia.fact: Saint Lucia has produced two Nobel Prize winners per capita, giving it the highest Nobel Prize rate of any country on Earth.

saint_vincent_and_the_grenadines.name: Saint Vincent and the Grenadines
saint_vincent_and_the_grenadines.fact: Saint Vincent was the filming location for Pirates of the Caribbean, which the island considers its greatest cultural contribution.

samoa.name: Samoa
samoa.fact: Samoa skipped the 29th of December 2011 entirely, jumping from Thursday to Saturday to align with its main trading partners.

san_marino.name: San Marino
san_marino.fact: San Marino claims to be the world's oldest republic, founded in 301 AD, and has survived being surrounded by Italy for 1,700 years through stubbornness.

sao_tome_and_principe.name: São Tomé and Príncipe
sao_tome_and_principe.fact: São Tomé and Príncipe straddles both the equator and the prime meridian, making it the closest country to coordinates 0,0 on Earth.

saudi_arabia.name: Saudi Arabia
saudi_arabia.fact: Saudi Arabia only allowed women to drive in 2018, a full century after most of the world, and announced it as a major progressive reform.

senegal.name: Senegal
senegal.fact: Senegal has never had a military coup in its entire history, making it a startling exception in a region where they are otherwise quite common.

serbia.name: Serbia
serbia.fact: Serbia is the world's second largest exporter of raspberries, which is a fact so surprising that most people assume it's wrong until they check.

seychelles.name: Seychelles
seychelles.fact: Seychelles has the world's smallest capital city by population with only 28,000 people, yet it still somehow manages to have traffic jams.

sierra_leone.name: Sierra Leone
sierra_leone.fact: Sierra Leone's capital is called Freetown because it was founded as a settlement for freed slaves, making it either a hopeful name or an ironic one.

singapore.name: Singapore
singapore.fact: Singapore fines you for chewing gum, jaywalking, and not flushing a public toilet, making it the world's most aggressively tidy city-state.

slovakia.name: Slovakia
slovakia.fact: Bratislava is one of the few capitals that borders two different countries — Austria and Hungary — making it conveniently located for day trips.

slovenia.name: Slovenia
slovenia.fact: Slovenia has the highest percentage of forest cover of any European country outside Finland and Sweden, yet somehow nobody talks about it.

solomon_islands.name: Solomon Islands
solomon_islands.fact: The Solomon Islands consists of 992 islands, most of which are unnamed, making its postal system an ongoing logistical adventure.

somalia.name: Somalia
somalia.fact: Somalia has been without a stable central government for so long it has accidentally become a case study in how societies function without states.

south_africa.name: South Africa
south_africa.fact: South Africa has three capital cities for executive, legislative and judicial functions, because choosing just one felt too decisive.

south_korea.name: South Korea
south_korea.fact: South Korea has the fastest average internet speeds in the world and considers slow Wi-Fi approximately a human rights issue.

south_sudan.name: South Sudan
south_sudan.fact: South Sudan is the world's newest country, gaining independence in 2011, and has since been working through a comprehensive list of nation-building challenges.

spain.name: Spain
spain.fact: Spain has a festival called La Tomatina where participants hurl 150,000 tomatoes at each other for no reason other than it being tremendous fun.

sri_lanka.name: Sri Lanka
sri_lanka.fact: Sri Lanka has the longest official capital name in the world at 22 characters, which is why most people just say Colombo and move on.

sudan.name: Sudan
sudan.fact: Khartoum sits at the exact confluence of the Blue Nile and White Nile, making it one of the most geographically significant cities in Africa.

suriname.name: Suriname
suriname.fact: Suriname is the smallest country in South America and the only Dutch-speaking country on the continent, which confuses people on two levels simultaneously.

sweden.name: Sweden
sweden.fact: Sweden has a law giving everyone the right to roam freely across any land including private property, which landowners have mixed feelings about.

switzerland.name: Switzerland
switzerland.fact: Switzerland has four official languages, a square national flag, and has stayed neutral in every war for 200 years, suggesting the Swiss have life figured out.

syria.name: Syria
syria.fact: Damascus is one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world, with settlement dating back 11,000 years, giving it a lot of context for current events.

taiwan.name: Taiwan
taiwan.fact: Taiwan produces over 90% of the world's most advanced semiconductor chips, making it one of the most geopolitically important small islands in history.

tajikistan.name: Tajikistan
tajikistan.fact: Tajikistan is 93% mountains, making it one of the most dramatic countries on Earth and an infrastructure engineer's worst nightmare.

tanzania.name: Tanzania
tanzania.fact: Tanzania is home to Kilimanjaro, which thousands of tourists attempt to climb each year despite having no mountaineering experience whatsoever.

thailand.name: Thailand
thailand.fact: Bangkok's full ceremonial name is 168 characters long and holds the Guinness record for longest city name, which Thais can recite and tourists cannot.

timor_leste.name: Timor-Leste
timor_leste.fact: Timor-Leste is one of the world's youngest countries, gaining independence in 2002, and has spent the years since demonstrating that nation-building is genuinely difficult.

togo.name: Togo
togo.fact: Togo is only 115km wide at its widest point, meaning you could drive across it sideways before finishing a podcast episode.

tonga.name: Tonga
tonga.fact: Tonga is the only Pacific nation never formally colonised by a European power, achieved through a combination of diplomacy and strategic timing.

trinidad_and_tobago.name: Trinidad and Tobago
trinidad_and_tobago.fact: Trinidad and Tobago invented the steel pan drum, the only new acoustic instrument family to be invented in the entire 20th century.

tunisia.name: Tunisia
tunisia.fact: Tunisia was the only country to emerge from the Arab Spring with a functioning democracy, which lasted about a decade before taking a complicated turn.

turkey.name: Turkey
turkey.fact: Turkey changed its name to Türkiye in 2022, partly to avoid association with the large bird that shares its old English name.

turkmenistan.name: Turkmenistan
turkmenistan.fact: Turkmenistan has a flaming gas crater called the Door to Hell that has been burning continuously since Soviet engineers accidentally set it on fire in 1971.

tuvalu.name: Tuvalu
tuvalu.fact: Tuvalu earns significant income by leasing its internet domain .tv to television companies, making it the only country whose economy depends on a clever URL.

uganda.name: Uganda
uganda.fact: Uganda has the youngest population of any country on Earth, with over half its citizens under 15 years old, giving it a very energetic national demographic.

ukraine.name: Ukraine
ukraine.fact: Ukraine is the largest country entirely within Europe and one of the world's biggest grain exporters, earning it the title breadbasket of Europe.

united_arab_emirates.name: United Arab Emirates
united_arab_emirates.fact: The UAE has a ski slope inside a shopping mall in the middle of a desert, which captures something essential about the country's philosophy.

united_kingdom.name: United Kingdom
united_kingdom.fact: The UK has an unwritten constitution, a monarch with theoretical powers nobody actually uses, and queues so orderly they're considered a cultural export.

united_states.name: United States
united_states.fact: The US is the only country to have used nuclear weapons in warfare and the only one to have landed on the moon, giving it a unique combination of achievements to reflect on.

uruguay.name: Uruguay
uruguay.fact: Uruguay was the first country to fully legalise marijuana at a national level in 2013, which neighbouring countries noted with interest.

uzbekistan.name: Uzbekistan
uzbekistan.fact: Uzbekistan is one of only two doubly landlocked countries in the world — surrounded entirely by other landlocked countries.

vanuatu.name: Vanuatu
vanuatu.fact: Vanuatu is the bungee jumping capital of the world, derived from a ritual where men jump from wooden towers with vines tied to their ankles.

vatican_city.name: Vatican City
vatican_city.fact: Vatican City publishes its own newspaper, radio station and TV channel for a population of about 800 people, which is an impressive media operation.

venezuela.name: Venezuela
venezuela.fact: Venezuela has the world's largest proven oil reserves yet experienced hyperinflation so severe its currency became essentially decorative.

vietnam.name: Vietnam
vietnam.fact: Vietnam defeated the French, the Americans and the Chinese in war within living memory, which gives its citizens a somewhat justified sense of confidence.

yemen.name: Yemen
yemen.fact: Sana'a is one of the highest capital cities in the world at 2,300 metres and one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities on Earth.

zambia.name: Zambia
zambia.fact: Zambia and Zimbabwe share Victoria Falls and have been arguing about the exact border through it ever since.

zimbabwe.name: Zimbabwe
zimbabwe.fact: Zimbabwe once issued a 100 trillion dollar banknote during hyperinflation, which was worth roughly enough to buy two loaves of bread.
